idea-plugin.name: Token Flow
idea-plugin.description:
Make using your tokens intuitive.
Token Flow helps you find the right variable and keep your styles flawless without ever leaving your IntelliJ code.

Token Flow indexes your whole Design System and makes token usage fluid, fast and error-proof.
Supported formats: SCSS variables, CSS Custom Properties, SCSS Maps, TS/JS preset objects, React Native runtime themes and helpers.

Superpowers for Front-end Developers

  • Instant Search (Library)
    A dedicated side panel to find tokens by name, value or category.
    Includes visual swatches and drag-and-drop support.
  • Smart Swapping (Alt+T)
    Need a slightly darker blue or the next spacing scale?
    Token Flow suggests siblings based on HSL proximity, logical order, or scale steps for callable helpers like spacing().
  • Zero "Magic Numbers"
    The IDE flags hardcoded hex codes, pixels or unitless numbers that match an existing token — one click and your code is clean.
  • Inline Suggestions
    As you type a value, matching tokens appear without stealing focus.
  • Deep Context
    Hover any token to see its resolved value, aliases and per-mode variants (Dark / Light / breakpoints).
  • Full-scale Audit
    An "Analyse" tab grades your project health.
    Identify tech debt, unused tokens and hardcoded clusters in seconds.

Real-world Use Cases

  • Rapid Integration — code a UI component without leaving the current file to check the token naming convention.
  • Theme Debugging — see instantly how a token resolves in Dark vs Light mode via the hover popup.
  • System Migration — refactor legacy CSS/SCSS by detecting every hardcoded value that should have been a token.
  • Consistency Check — keep multi-brand projects aligned with Scopes to isolate tokens per platform or sub-project.

Made by

Robin Lopez — designer & front-end engineer.
robinlopez.fr · LinkedIn · Bluesky · Bento
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.4 — Visual Grid & Polished UX

  • Design System Dashboard - Grid View: New visual mode representing tokens as beautiful, interactive cards. Includes visual previews for colors, spacing, and radius.
  • Smarter Categorization: Added 6 new categories (EFFECTS, LAYOUT, BORDER, etc.) and refined the engine to flawlessly classify complex semantic tokens.
  • Contextual Scope Awareness: The Dashboard and Analyser now dynamically track and display your currently edited file's scope.

0.1.3 — Gutter swatches & SCSS modifiers

  • Gutter color swatches for SCSS: variables ($name) now display a color preview icon in the gutter, resolving through the project index (consistent with native CSS variables).
  • Robust SCSS/CSS parsing: modifiers like !default, !global, and !important are now stripped during extraction, fixing color previews and alias resolution for flagged tokens.

0.1.2 — React Native support

  • React Native runtime themes: property-access tokens (colors.PRIMARY_500, theme.radius.sm, fontPresets.h1.fontSize) are now detected. Typed export const X: Type = {…} aggregators contribute leaf paths only (aligned with barrel re-exports); bare const X = {…} bags keep their prefix. Cross-object alias resolution included.
  • Callable helpers (spacing, radius, …): linear arrow functions like (v) => UNIT * v are indexed with a ƒ badge. Hardcoded 12 now suggests spacing(1.5). Alt+T on spacing(0.5) opens a scale popup (spacing(0.25), spacing(0.5), spacing(1), spacing(1.5), …).
  • Unitless numerics flagged: fontSize: 34, lineHeight: 24, opacity: 0.5 now inspected against the token index (JS/TS only). Numbers inside a helper call are ignored to avoid noise.
  • Suggest matching tokens as you type: the value-completion popup no longer steals editor focus — typing keeps going into the file.
  • Analyser respects the selected scope: file walk is restricted to the scope's rootPath; only the active scope's own catalogues are excluded. Scope changes in Settings refresh the picker live; a Re-sync action is available for manual cache drops.
  • Architecture: TS/JS parsing reorganised into a strategy package (StyleDictionaryParser, RuntimeObjectParser, RuntimeFunctionParser, registry to dispatch per file). Adding a new stack now means plugging in a single class.

0.1.1 — Polish & bug fixes

  • Hover popup — multi-theme grouping: tokens declared under nested SCSS maps ($themes-config -> "themeOne" -> "light" -> …) now render with theme-grouped column headers instead of a flat default | dark | light | dark sequence.
  • Settings — Scopes tab: long intro hints no longer trigger a horizontal scrollbar when the dialog is narrow; HTML paragraphs wrap properly inside the available width.
  • Marketplace metadata cleanup (vendor contact, simplified description).

0.1.0 — First public release

The first iteration of Token Flow ships everything you need to manage your Design System directly inside the IDE.

  • Multi-format indexer — SCSS, CSS, and TS/JS support with alias resolution.
  • Library Tool Window — Visual search, drag-and-drop, and navigation.
  • Alternatives Popup (Alt+T) — Smart sorting for colors and metrics.
  • Hardcoded Inspection — Automatic detection and quick-fix for literals.
  • Analyse Tab — Deep health report and technical debt tracking.
  • Scopes — Multi-UI project support.

localInspection.displayName: Hardcoded value matches a design token

action.description: Scans the project and lists every detected design token
Shows alternatives of the design token under the caret
Opens the file where the token under the caret is declared
